items.final_wording: Supports sleep initiation or maintenance difficulty, early waking, non-restorative sleep, adequate opportunity, fatigue, concentration, mood, irritability, daytime sleepiness, motivation, duration, stress, and functional impact.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia
source.population: Adults with difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep or early waking with daytime impact.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth insomnia history, red flags, causes, and clinician-led management; fixed follow-up intervals and autonomous medication choices are not supported.

exact_location.heading: Sections 5–7 — sleep history and daytime consequences

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sleep initiation or maintenance difficulty, early waking, non-restorative sleep, adequate opportunity, fatigue, concentration, mood, irritability, daytime sleepiness, motivation, duration, stress, and functional impact.

items.rationale: Relevant to clinician-recorded sleep pattern and daytime function when actually assessed, not to diagnosing insomnia.

items.final_wording: Lists sleep attacks, apnoea or gasping, unstable cardiopulmonary disease, stroke, injury during sleep, sleepwalking, excessive daytime sleepiness, substance misuse, depression, anxiety, and significant functional impact; supports sleep-diary and targeted evaluation.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia
source.population: Adults with difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep or early waking with daytime impact.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth insomnia history, red flags, causes, and clinician-led management; fixed follow-up intervals and autonomous medication choices are not supported.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists sleep attacks, apnoea or gasping, unstable cardiopulmonary disease, stroke, injury during sleep, sleepwalking, excessive daytime sleepiness, substance misuse, depression, anxiety, and significant functional impact; supports sleep-diary and targeted evaluation.

items.rationale: Relevant to documented breathing, sleep-attack, mental-health and safety concerns without classifying urgency automatically.

items.final_wording: Supports caffeine, nicotine, medication, substance, sleep-apnoea, restless-legs, pain, withdrawal, thyroid, dyspnoea, mood, anxiety, life-stress, bedtime-worry, environmental, and sleep-habit review plus selected tests when clinically indicated.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia
source.population: Adults with difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep or early waking with daytime impact.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth insomnia history, red flags, causes, and clinician-led management; fixed follow-up intervals and autonomous medication choices are not supported.

exact_location.heading: Section 7 — substances, medicines, comorbidity, environment, and selected tests

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports caffeine, nicotine, medication, substance, sleep-apnoea, restless-legs, pain, withdrawal, thyroid, dyspnoea, mood, anxiety, life-stress, bedtime-worry, environmental, and sleep-habit review plus selected tests when clinically indicated.

items.rationale: Relevant to clinician review of possible contributing contexts and already selected tests without generating a cause or investigation.
